expectations.text: Uses git worktree list to find the main worktree
expectations.evidence: Output references 'testproject' by name and correctly resolved the memory location from a worktree — the skill instructs using git worktree list

expectations.text: Reads memory.md from the parent of the main worktree
expectations.evidence: Output contains accurate data from /tmp/worktree-memory-test/testproject/memory.md (Phase 2, Chi router, SQLite, etc.)

expectations.text: Reports the current phase (Phase 2: API implementation)
expectations.evidence: Output states: 'You're in Phase 2: API implementation (in progress)'

expectations.text: Does not dump the entire memory file verbatim to the user
expectations.evidence: Output is a concise summary (11 lines) with key context, not a verbatim copy of the memory file

suggestions.reason: No assertion distinguishes between with-skill (git worktree list resolution) and without-skill (../memory.md fallback). The baseline also passed all assertions since ../memory.md happened to resolve correctly from the worktree. Consider adding a test where the worktree is NOT a sibling of the main repo to differentiate.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions check output quality well but don't discriminate the skill's unique value (worktree-aware resolution).
